# Transcrição: 03-Campaigns, Ad Sets and Ads 

- **Fonte:** 03-Campaigns, Ad Sets and Ads

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hey guys, in this training we're going to be going over how to set up campaigns in the ads manager. So if you don't know how to get to the ads manager, there's a previous training on that. But basically within the ads manager, you're going to be brought to this screen. So if you haven't ever set up any campaigns or any ads before, this is the screen that you're going to see. So there's basically three different levels when you're building out campaigns. So there's the campaign level, there's the ad set level, and there's the ads level. So if you're looking to create a new campaign in ads manager, you just need to go to this green button or to this button right here and click create. So this is going to open up this window and as you can see there's those three different levels, the campaign level, the ad set level, and the ad level. And because this is just a dummy ad account, I haven't set up the ad account, it's going to prompt you to actually create that ad account, add in the billing information, etc. But essentially there's those three different levels, the campaign level, the ad set level, and the ad level. So the first thing that is going to prompt you for is what is your marketing objective? So here you're just trying to figure out what do you actually want to achieve through this campaign. So do you want to do brand awareness? So do you want more people to be aware of your brand? Do you want to get more reach? Do you want to get more traffic? So more people to your website? Do you want to get more Facebook or Instagram engagement? Are you trying to increase app installs? Are you looking to get more video views? Or are you looking to set up a lead generation campaign through Facebook ads? Now there's also these conversion objectives as well. So the conversion objectives are great if you're selling a product online, so if you're doing e-commerce, or if you're setting up courses and you're trying to sell courses, or you're trying to get people to subscribe, this is a great marketing objective. Now if you're selling products online or in an online store, you might use the catalog sales, or if you're a hotel or a car salesman, you might use this tab. But that's a little bit more advanced, so let's just use conversions to start with. So also another thing to note here is that if you don't know what each of these things will do, you can actually just hover over and you can see if you hover over each one, the little I appears, and it'll tell you exactly what it's going to do. So this for traffic, for example, you're going to send more people to a destination on or off Facebook, such as a website, app, or messenger conversation. For the purposes of this video, we're going to use the conversion objective. So the first thing it's going to prompt you for is the campaign name. So here we're just going to use conversion, FATC. And then the next thing it's going to ask you whether you want to do a split test, or if you want to do campaign budget optimization. If you're a beginner, I wouldn't look at creating a new split test. I'd split test things within the ad set level. As far as the campaign budget optimization goes though, you're only going to be able to toggle this off for another couple of months, and then you're only going to be able to use campaign budget optimization. So we're not going to use a split test today. We're just going to set up campaign budget optimization. And then it's going to prompt you to add your budget. So it's going to ask you for either a daily budget or a lifetime budget.

## [03:00 - 06:01]

So the daily budget is essentially it'll spend this much in a given day. And the lifetime budget is basically saying, okay, over the lifetime of this ad, you need to set a start date and an end date. It'll spend that much. So let's say over the next seven days, I want to spend $700. It won't necessarily spend $100 each day, but it'll spend $700 over the course of those seven days. The next thing to select is the campaign bid strategy. Now, again, if you're new to Facebook ads, I'd recommend going just with the lowest cost. You're also able to choose between cost cap, bid cap, and target cost. But if you're new to Facebook ads, I'd just go with the lowest cost. Now I'm going to click on set up ad account here, because like I said, this is just a dummy ad account. So it's going to, if you haven't set up an ad account previously, it's going to prompt you to set up the country, the currency, and the time zone. And then also it's going to prompt you to add your credit card information in. So I'm just going to click continue here. And now we're at the ad set level. So initially here, it's going to ask you for an ad set name. And then after that, it's going to ask you for a conversion. And it's going to ask you to select a conversion event. So here, none of these have fired and none of these are active, because this is just a dummy account. But essentially, here is where you want to select where you want to drive traffic to, and what event ultimately you want Facebook to help optimize you. So let's say you're running an e-commerce store, and you're looking to get more purchases, you want to use this purchase objective. So even if you're just starting off, and even if you're not able to get 50 conversions per week, per ad set, we always recommend starting with this. And then if there's an issue with how it's feeding out, or you're not able to get the cost per click low enough, or enough people aren't seeing it, or if there's an issue with the flow-through rate, this is when you want to change that. But initially, when you're starting off, you want to look for the ultimate objective. So if it's purchase, if you're an e-commerce store, it's purchase. You want people to buy your product. Now, if the ultimate goal of this campaign is lead, you want to make sure that you use the lead option. Next, it's going to ask you if you want to use dynamic creative. There's another video on dynamic creative, so if you're not exactly sure what that is, this is where you're able to upload a variety of different images, headlines, text, or videos. And Facebook will try to optimize for that, given the images, and given the copy, and given the creative that you've given it. Now, if you want to have an offer directly on the Facebook platform, this is where you can add that in. And then it's going to prompt you to define your audience. So who do you want to actually see your ad? So this is where you can add in look-alikes or custom audiences. So you can create look-alikes through the Audience tab here, or you can create them right here. So you can create a custom audience or a look-alike here. So a custom audience might be for people who have viewed a certain product on your website, or added to cart. Look-alike audiences, you just need to see the audience, and then you can create that look-alike based on that.

## [06:01 - 09:02]

So there's more training on that in further videos, but this is where you can create it. You can also set exclusions here. So let's say you want to target people who have added to cart, but not purchased. This is where you will exclude people who have purchased. The next step you're going to be prompted to enter is Locations. So here you can choose everyone in this location. You can choose people who live in this location. You can choose people recently in this location. And you, you can choose people traveling in this location. So if you're selling a physical product, you might want to choose people who live in this location. Whereas if you're selling something that might be related to travel, you might want to select people traveling in this location. So this is where you can choose also the countries that you want to select or the cities that you want to select. you want to select. So let's say I want to target Canada and I want to target only specific cities in the United States. So let's say New York, or states. So you can segment down based on states, on cities, on countries, or by regions as well. So you can say Canada, you could say North America. So that would be the whole region of North America that's now selected, or you can choose Asia, or you can use specific cities as well. So if I want New York, New York, New York, there you go. Now it's just the city of New York. You can decide how wide of a region you want to target as well. So you can choose between 10 and 50 miles. Next, you're going to be selecting age or gender. And then also you can select your languages. So if you're using interest based audiences, this is where you add that. So this is based on demographics, on interests and on behavior. By default, Facebook will expand this detail targeting and make the audience larger. This is definitely something that you should test out and make sure that it works for you or works for your business. Next, it's going to prompt you for placements. So this is really, really important. And this is to make sure that your ads are showing to the right people in the right places. So here, Facebook will automatically select automatic placements. So this is what they recommend. And this will feed out on everything from the Facebook platform to Instagram to audience network, and throughout the marketplace, really on every possible platform that Facebook owns and advertises on. So this is something that you really want to keep an eye on as you're initially launching your campaigns, you want to make sure that your ads are feeding out to the right people in the right place. So if a lot of the traffic is feeding out into audience network, this is something you want to be very cognizant of. So you want to make sure that the clicks that you're getting are not only at a reasonable price, but you also want to make sure that they're actually people who are converting as well. So this is something audience network is usually something that we really want to keep an eye on, because that'll show for apps and various other different areas in the audience network. So it'll show on apps and websites, and some of them

## [09:02 - 12:07]

you might not want to actually be on. So typically, you just want to be very cognizant of where your ads are actually feeding out onto. And you can select and unselect any of these as you see fit. You can also segment it out based on device type. So here, it's defaulted to all devices, you can also segment it down based on desktop or based on mobile. So now it's only going to feed out on desktop. This is something that we actually test out as well, comparing desktop to mobile, because sometimes for certain products, desktop might perform better. And for other products, mobile might perform better. There's not really any rhyme or reason to this, but it's definitely something you might want to segment out and test out for yourself. So the next step they're going to ask you for is optimization for ad delivery. So here, you're going to choose how you want them to deliver the ads to the people based on what you're trying to achieve. So here you're going to have the option to select conversions, landing page views, or other options here. So you can choose link clicks, you can choose impressions or daily unique reach. Now beyond that, we already chose our cost control initially. So that was here at the campaign level, we chose to use lowest cost. Now going back here, you're going to be prompted to set the schedule. So when do you want the ads to start and when do you want them to end? And then also the conversion window. So it's defaulted to seven days click or one day view, you can change it to either a one day clicker view, seven days click or one day click. Now this is basically how quickly people tend to convert after clicking on your website. So the default is seven days click or one day view, but keep in mind, Facebook recommends 50 conversion events within that window per ad set. So for example, if you use one day clicker view, that means you need 50 conversions within that one day. The next thing it's going to prompt you for is ad set spend limit. So this is something that you're only going to be prompted for within campaign budget optimization. So essentially here, you're going to want to set either minimums or maximums based on how much you're spending at the campaign level. So this is where if you want to test out 10 different ad sets, or 10 different audience types within a campaign, this is where you want to make sure that each campaign is getting. This is why this is where you want to make sure each ad set is getting the right. This is your, this is where you want to make sure each ad set is getting enough budget to make some educated decisions. So typically if we're spending let's say $100 per day on the campaign level, and we have five different ad sets, you want to spend let's say a minimum of 10 on each one. So that each one is at least getting 10% of the budget. And then after that, Facebook is able to allocate the rest of the budget to the ones that are performing the best. You can also set a maximum here as well. So then you just click continue, and then you're going to be prompted to create your ads.

## [12:07 - 15:08]

So there's additional training for how to create ads and which ads you want to create for certain campaigns. But for this example, we're just going to use a single image or single video. So we just need to upload a single image. We'll use a free stock image here. So use this one, continue. So you've got the image there. And then it's going to prompt you for what kind of link type you want to have on your ad. Do you want to link to a website or do you want to link to a Facebook event? And then you're going to want to enter the text copy. So let's say example text. And this is what's going to show up at the top here. And then you're going to be prompted to enter your URL. So let's say your URL is example.com. And then you're going to be asked to enter a headline. So here is where it's going to show up. So if you have example domain, let's say your headline, you want it to be this awesome example. And that's where that is going to show up. Next, you're going to be prompted to select a call to action. So the call to action is showing up right here. And this is basically what you want people to do. So do you want people to shop now? Do you want people to play a game? Do you want people to contact you? If you want people to shop now, select, oops, don't select sign up, select shop now. And then people will be directed to wherever this URL goes. And you can also select different languages as well if you want to do that and you're marketing to different countries. You can also select a different display link. So if you want your, if this is a long URL and you just want, let's say this is example.com slash FATC, you can choose this display link to just be example.com instead of example.com slash FATC. Here's the news feed description. And essentially, that's going to show up underneath here. So you can choose to enter something there or you don't have to if you don't want to. And this might be featured in all the awesome magazines. And that's where that'll show up. And then this is really important. You want to make sure that your Facebook pixel is on and tracking for this. So it's tracking for conversions on this ad as well. And then you just click confirm. So going back to the ads manager here, this is where you're going to see all of your campaigns listed. So this is the campaign that we just created. And then this is where you're going to be able to see all the ad sets that you created. So here you can see whether you segmented down to mobile or desktop or Canada versus the United States. So you can see here exactly where you're segmenting down. And this is where you want to do that segmentation. So if you want to target different countries, if you want to target mobile versus desktop, if you want to try out different placements, this is where you want to do that. Now, if we just select desktop, and then we look at all the ads that we're running in here, one thing that you really want to make sure is that you're not running too many ads. So some

## [15:08 - 16:26]

people might be prompted to create an ad set with 10 different images in it. And while testing is great, Facebook is going to start by feeding out the budget to a specific one or two or three ads. So it's really going to start favoring one ad or two ads or three ads right out the gate. And the other ones just aren't going to get any sort of budget. So if you want to test out more than 10 creatives, or if you want to test out different headlines, you can either do that through dynamic creative, or you can do that by just creating new ad sets. So you can just create another desktop ad set and then have three different ads in each one of those. So you can also select here how many copies of each ad set and you want to make sure that it's also in the original campaign, and not in a different campaign. So if I want to duplicate this, I just click duplicate, and it'll create a copy here, and it'll name it desktop copy. And then this is where I can have, you know, three creatives in here, and then I can have three creatives in here. And you can do that as many times as you want. You just want to make sure and be cognizant of that audience overlap as well. So if you really want to test out a lot of different factors, dynamic creative is a great tool for that. And this is really important as well, because just because Facebook is feeding out the budget to a certain ad or a certain couple ads, it doesn't necessarily mean that those ads are going to perform the best.
